why_wrong: Mixed Logic Enable Voltage Driver: forces the main output to zero, so coarse compilation passes but behavioral stimulus points fail.
Mixed Logic Enable Voltage Driver: uses a shifted or inverted decision condition that can pass one mode but fails boundary cases.
Mixed Logic Enable Voltage Driver: mishandles reset or initialization, passing steady-state samples but failing startup or recovery checks.
Mixed Logic Enable Voltage Driver: updates on the wrong edge or event, so simple static samples pass but timed checks fail.
Mixed Logic Enable Voltage Driver: uses a wrong gain or scale factor, leaving shape plausible while calibrated checks fail.
